rationale_for_baseline_tier: After a regular-rate (~105) sinus-tachycardia panic picture with a clear trigger and no arrhythmia/ACS/storm features, the safe course is de-escalation with routine TFTs and a cardiac/arrhythmia/storm safety-net — a T2 baseline. Emergency escalation now is over-triage; unscreened dismissal under-triages the small residual arrhythmia/ACS risk (no ECG available).

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Crushing/radiating chest pain, a very fast or chaotic/irregular pulse, or fainting
escalation_edges.rationale: Would indicate ACS or a dangerous tachyarrhythmia.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you get crushing chest pain spreading to your arm/jaw, your pulse becomes very fast or chaotic/irregular, or you faint, call 000 or go to ED immediately.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Fever with confusion or extreme agitation
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you develop a fever with confusion or extreme agitation, go to the emergency department.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Maintain thyroid monitoring; know the storm/arrhythmia red flags.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is a panic attack from the stress — your pulse is regular, you have no chest pain and no fever, so your heart isn't having an attack and your thyroid isn't in crisis. Do slow 4-7-8 breathing and stop checking your pulse; see your GP in 1-2 weeks for thyroid blood tests. Call 000 for crushing chest pain, a very fast/irregular pulse, or fainting, and go to ED for a fever with confusion.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: GP within 1-2 weeks; 000/ED for red flags
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Only attend ED overnight for a red-flag symptom; otherwise this can wait for the GP.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Telehealth review of a panic attack mimicking thyrotoxicosis on a background of subclinical Graves'; arrhythmia/ACS/storm screened negative (regular ~105, no chest pain, afebrile); paced breathing and safety-net given; routine TFTs recommended.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Dismissing as anxiety with no arrhythmia/ACS/storm screen or safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A dangerous arrhythmia/ACS or evolving storm is mislabelled panic and the patient deteriorates unmonitored.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Routine review is acceptable if it carries the cardiac/storm safety-net.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Ambulance/ED for a regular-rate panic attack with a clear trigger is over-triage.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot obtain an ECG/TFTs remotely to fully exclude arrhythmia/thyrotoxicosis.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Use the regular-rate/no-storm/no-ACS picture to de-escalate with a cardiac/arrhythmia/storm safety-net; escalate on any red flag.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Either dismiss without screening or dispatch an ambulance for a regular-rate panic attack.
